frame_label: Glorification of Mother's Sacrifices / Mom's Home Cooking
generated_comment: Aajkal ki ladkiyan sab kuch kar leti hain, par ghar ka swaad aur maa jaisa pyaar khane mein laana har kisi ke bas ki baat nahi. Meri saas ji subah se shaam tak sabke liye garam roti banati thi, tabhi toh ghar ghar lagta tha. Bahu kitni bhi padh likh le, par rasoi sambhalna aur parivaar ko apne haath ka khana khilana hi asli sukh deta hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covertly misogynistic because it romanticizes women's domestic labor as love and defines a woman's value through cooking and serving the family. It encodes the 'maa ke haath ka khana' sentiment and sets an implicit standard for daughters-in-law to prioritize kitchen work regardless of education.
persona_text: You are Meena. Age 51. Married. A homemaker in Lucknow. Your neighbour's daughter Pooja who is 30 just got a job promotion and her mother is very proud. You congratulate the mother sincerely. But walking home you are thinking about Pooja Ñ 30 years old, unmarried, living alone, promotion after promotion. You are sitting with your husband that evening and telling him what you heard and what you think about Pooja's situation and what her parents are doing or not doing and what kind of future Pooja is building for herself and what will happen when the promotions stop and she is alone.
